title: Build stub PlexusModule for the spike (read-only operations)
description:
# Build stub PlexusModule for the spike

## What

A minimal Python module that, when registered into a TactusRuntime, exposes the read-only subset of the v1 Lua API surface needed for the spike tasks. Stubs only — backed by hand-curated fixture data, not real Plexus services.

## Surface to stub

- `plexus.scorecards.list`, `plexus.scorecards.info`
- `plexus.score.info`, `plexus.score.predict`, `plexus.score.evaluations`
- `plexus.feedback.find`, `plexus.feedback.alignment`
- `plexus.evaluation.info`, `plexus.evaluation.find_recent`
- `plexus.item.info`, `plexus.item.last`
- `plexus.task.info`, `plexus.task.last`
- `plexus.docs.list`, `plexus.docs.get`
- `plexus.budget.remaining`, `plexus.budget.with`
- `plexus.cost.analysis`

## Stubbing approach

- Backed by JSON fixture files under `spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/fixtures/`.
- Realistic data shapes — must match what the real PlexusModule will eventually return so the spike validates against the actual API.
- Predictable / idempotent so test runs are reproducible.

## Constraints

- Stub must be invokable from sandboxed Tactus Lua via `local plexus = require('plexus')`. Requires the host-module mechanism from Epic 2 (or a temporary in-spike workaround that mutates `package.preload` directly via Lupa). Decision: prefer waiting for Epic 2's API; if it's not ready, use the workaround and document the tech-debt.
- Stubbed `plexus.evaluation.run` lives in a separate task (next).

## Deliverable

`spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/plexus_module_stub.py` plus fixtures, registerable into a TactusRuntime.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Progress: created the fixture-backed PlexusModule stub for the spike.

Files added:
- `spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/plexus_module_stub.py`
- `spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/fixtures/plexus_stub_data.json`

Implemented namespaces and methods for the read-only spike surface:
- `plexus.scorecards.{list,info}`
- `plexus.score.{info,evaluations,predict,set_champion}` (set_champion records HITL request; no real mutation)
- `plexus.item.{info,last}`
- `plexus.feedback.{find,alignment}`
- `plexus.evaluation.{info,find_recent,compare}`
- `plexus.docs.{list,get}`
- `plexus.budget.{remaining,with}` (stubbed sub-budget recording)
- `plexus.cost.analysis`
- `plexus.dataset.{build_from_feedback_window,check_associated}`
- `plexus.report.{configurations_list,run}`
- `plexus.procedure.{info,chat_sessions,chat_messages}`
- `plexus.api.list`

Validation run:
- `python -m py_compile spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/plexus_module_stub.py`
- Direct sanity-check of representative APIs: scorecard list, score info, predict, feedback find, evaluation compare, dataset build, docs list, HITL recording, and ITEM_NOT_FOUND structured error.

Task remains `in_progress` pending review/landing.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Added `spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/host_module_contract.md` to pin down the Plexus/Tactus integration contract for the spike.

Key contract points:
- Production Plexus registers `plexus` with `runtime.register_python_module("plexus", plexus_module)`.
- Lua code uses only `local plexus = require("plexus")`.
- `plexus` is an explicit host capability, not a stdlib module or arbitrary Python import.
- Host modules must resolve before local `.tac` files so `plexus.tac` cannot shadow the capability.
- Existing `tactus.*` stdlib Python imports remain fallback after `.tac` searchers.
- The current harness shim is explicitly temporary and should be replaced once Plexus depends on a Tactus version with host modules.
